pull.title: Verify `compressed` argument in `secp256k1_eckey_pubkey_serialize`
pull.body: Due to similarity to the public API function `secp256k1_ec_pubkey_serialize`, public API flags like `SECP256K1_EC_COMPRESSED` are sometimes mistakingly passed to `secp256k1_eckey_pubkey_serialize` in newly proposed code (this is currently the case for several modules in secp256k1-zkp, see https://github.com/BlockstreamResearch/secp256k1-zkp/pull/300), which is currently not detected. To avoid this in the future, a VERIFY_CHECK is added to check that the `compressed` argument is either 0 or 1.

repo.name: secp256k1
repo.description: Optimized C library for EC operations on curve secp256k1

repo.name: secp256k1
repo.description: Optimized C library for EC operations on curve secp256k1

events.message:
Verify `compressed` argument in `secp256k1_eckey_pubkey_serialize`

Due to similarity to the public API function `secp256k1_ec_pubkey_serialize`,
public API flags like `SECP256K1_EC_COMPRESSED` are sometimes mistakingly
passed to newly proposed code (this is currently the case for several modules in
secp256k1-zkp, see https://github.com/BlockstreamResearch/secp256k1-zkp/pull/300).
which is currently not detected. To avoid this in the future, a VERIFY_CHECK
is added to check that the `compressed` argument is either 0 or 1.

events.body: ACK 1823594. Got tests failures when passing public API flags to `secp256k1_eckey_pubkey_serialize`.

comments.body: In other places, we simply rely on C's int-as-bool convention, so I suggest adding a comment that explains why we enforce 0/1 in this case.
